required_skills: Low-level C++ programming with deep expertise in memory management, cache optimization, and multi-threaded systems
Real-time rendering pipeline development including rasterization, deferred rendering, and physically-based rendering (PBR)
Game engine architecture design covering core systems: entity management, scene graph, resource management, and serialization
GPU programming and shader development using HLSL, GLSL, or SPIR-V across DirectX 12, Vulkan, or Metal APIs
Multi-threading and job system architecture for parallel game engine workloads on modern multi-core hardware
Memory allocation strategies including custom allocators, memory pools, and stack allocators for performance-critical engine systems
Platform-specific optimization and porting for PC, console (PlayStation, Xbox, Nintendo Switch), and mobile hardware
Profiling and low-level performance analysis using GPU/CPU profilers to eliminate rendering bottlenecks and frame budget overruns
preferred_skills: Ray tracing pipeline implementation using DirectX Raytracing (DXR) or Vulkan Ray Tracing extensions
Physics engine integration or development including rigid body dynamics, constraint solvers, and broadphase collision detection
Audio engine architecture including spatial audio, DSP pipeline design, and low-latency audio mixing systems
Custom scripting language design or integration (e.g., Lua embedding) for designer-facing engine tooling
Procedural mesh generation, terrain streaming, and level-of-detail (LOD) systems for open-world game environments
Temporal Anti-Aliasing (TAA), DLSS, FSR, and other upscaling and image quality techniques
Game engine tooling development including custom editors, asset pipeline tools, and hot-reload systems
responsibilities: Design and implement core engine subsystems including rendering pipelines, scene management, resource streaming, and job scheduling architectures
Develop real-time rendering features such as deferred shading, shadow mapping, ambient occlusion, global illumination, and post-processing effects using DirectX 12 or Vulkan
Build and optimize multi-threaded job systems and task schedulers that maximize CPU utilization across all available cores on target hardware platforms
Implement custom memory management systems including frame allocators, pool allocators, and defragmentation strategies to minimize garbage collection and allocation stalls
Write HLSL or GLSL shaders for complex rendering effects, collaborating with technical artists to deliver visually stunning and performant materials
Profile and optimize engine performance using GPU debuggers (RenderDoc, PIX) and CPU profilers (VTune), delivering solutions that maintain stable 60fps or 30fps frame budgets
Port engine systems to multiple hardware platforms (PC, PlayStation, Xbox, Nintendo Switch), handling platform-specific API differences, memory constraints, and certification requirements
Develop internal engine tooling and editor extensions that improve the productivity of gameplay programmers, technical artists, and level designers
Conduct rigorous code reviews for engine-level pull requests, enforcing coding standards, architectural patterns, and performance contracts
Research and integrate emerging rendering technologies such as mesh shaders, ray tracing (DXR), DLSS, FSR, and hardware-accelerated physics into the production engine pipeline
